title: iv>
content:
iv>


Day 3
MORNING
  • Morning Reading: "He crouched in the searing sun, his skin burning, felt pain, felt thirst, felt fatigue. And he crouched in the rain, water pouring from his hair over his freezing shoulders, over his freezing hips and legs. And the ascetic froze and shivered, until his knees and joints rattled."
  • Today's Intention: I will notice the difference between disciplined practice and self-punishment in my thoughts and actions today.

Answer: Today I will keep on trying to live for myself. I will discipline myself further by not giving into the temptation of the comfortable past. The toxic cycle that occurs between Kevin and I. I will be better today. I will think of one activity that I’d enjoy and Kevin would too that would not harm either of us, which we both could do together to have a positive effect on our bond, instead of it ending in an argument or any negative feelings, something good will have come of it.


NIGHT
  • Night Reading: "He lost his Self a thousand times and for days on end he dwelt in non-being. But though the paths took him away from Self, in the end they always led back to it."
context: From document: A 30-Day Journey with Siddhartha: Aligning the Path with Daily Life — chunk 9 (chunk 9/29)
